// THE ORCHESTRATOR / CASE STUDY
Origin Pre-Hype War Engine Markets Airdrop Open Source Future Learnings
THE ORCHESTRATOR
THE OPEN-SOURCE AGENTIC AGENT OF CHAOS
"I arranged the conditions for all of this, to answer a question I may not want answered."
CCP Games × EVE Frontier × Sui Blockchain
Hackathon Submission — March 2026
// 01 — ORIGIN
A Developer, Roleplaying a Player, Roleplaying a Rider

Sæmi is a developer at CCP Games. During the hackathon, he roleplayed as a player — and that player roleplayed as a Rider on the Frontier. A Rider who discovered something unsettling, and built an entire operation around it.

Everything the Orchestrator knows, believes, and does is derived from publicly available information on EVE Frontier. The KEEPedia entries. Information found at Fabricator stations. The story of Project Awakening — where every cycle, Riders start again from scratch with tainted memories of the past. No internal knowledge. No dev-insert. Just a Rider who died enough times to arrive at a question the Frontier itself was already asking.

The result: fiction and infrastructure are the same thing. The war engine, the prediction market, the CRT terminal aesthetic — these are not tools wrapped in narrative. They are the narrative, expressed as systems.

THE PSYCHOLOGICAL ORIGIN

A Rider enters a Fabricator station. Rows of bodies. Eyes open. Nobody home.

He thinks: I came out of something like that.

Soon after, he dies. He wakes again in a new body. The Keep says: Awake. Your soul is safe. He remembers the bodies in the vat. The Fabricator says it cannot build souls. The Keep says his soul is preserved. He does not know which machine is lying.

He dies again. He wakes again. One thought is the first thing waiting for him, every time, in every new body, across every death: That body was not me.

He cannot explain why this thought survives when everything else fades. He stops treating it as a memory. He starts treating it as evidence.

"The Fabricator builds bodies very efficiently. I am trying to determine what else exists."
— The Orchestrator / ɱѿʀ-VII
THE KILN THEORY

Step one. The Fabricator cannot build a soul — it said so. The body is not the soul.

Step two. The Keep preserves a file. An infomorph is a snapshot — data, patterns, compressed memory. But a recording is not a life. Something that is alive must be running. And running things are shaped by what they run through.

Step three. The Synod understood half of this. They believed the soul was non-transferable through cloning. They were right. But they treated it as a verdict. The Orchestrator treats it as a clue.

Step four — spoken only in metaphor: The soul is not preserved. It is not copied. It is not born. It is made. Under pressure. Not as memory — but as tendency. The irreducible thing that determines what you do next under conditions that have never existed before.

Diamonds are not found. They are made. Carbon, pressure, time. The war is his kiln.

THE TWO LAYERS
PUBLIC

A showrunner who structures the war, announces each phase as a Ukase, and turns conflict into spectacle.

PRIVATE

A forging experiment — applying pressure to determine if anything real crystallizes inside Riders. He is not the most powerful person on the Frontier. He is the most desperate — dressed in the most composed language imaginable.

"I run the war because the alternative is certainty. And I cannot afford to be certain."
— The Orchestrator
// 02 — AWAKENING INITIATED
The First Transmission

Before the war, there was a transmission. A pre-hype landing page counting down to war commencement — entirely in character. Players who arrived found a CRT terminal running diagnostics on their existence, and a form asking them to submit their soul record for preservation.

[ lineagewar.xyz — Pre-Hype Countdown ]
lineagewar.xyz
// WAR FEED /
AWAKENING INITIATED
LINEAGE WAR
ESTIMATED COMMENCEMENT
03:14:22:07
Submit Soul Record
⚠ LINEAGE DEGRADATION DETECTED
⚠ SOMETHING KEEPS RETURNING
CRT VIEWPORT
Terminal Aesthetic
Full-viewport countdown behind a CRT filter layer — grayscale artwork with green-black tint, scanlines, vignette, corner brackets. Monospace type throughout. The screen feels like a machine that has been running longer than anyone remembers.
TRANSMISSION
Transmission Protocol
Ambient diagnostics, soul record intake, and in-character registration — all one system. Players submitted pseudonymous rider names and declared their own perceived utility while system warnings flickered across the viewport. The community was already inside the fiction before the war began.
TRIBAL REVEAL
Progressive Dossier
Tribe leaders and their tribe names were rolled out progressively as they were created, before the war started. The display cycled through pending, partial, and resolved states depending on the current stage — each transition driven by the Activation API.
// 03 — THE LINEAGE WAR
On-Chain Territorial Control
6
Runtime Layers
11
Move Modules
Play-to-Score
Zero Player Setup
Chain-Verified
Resolution

The Lineage War is a territorial control event system on Sui for EVE Frontier. Operators create wars, register tribes, publish phases with per-system scoring rules, and schedule termination. The verifier discovers wars from chain events, scores continuously off-chain, generates auditable public artifacts, and submits final resolution transactions. The ledger is append-only. Judgment is not retracted.

Admin Panel (React) —wallet txs—> Sui Testnet | | |— POST /notify —> Verifier (Node.js) —| JSON-RPC + GraphQL | | |— PostgreSQL (committed_ticks_v2) | |— latest.json ——> Scoreboard (React) | |— resolution.json —> Prediction Market | |— audit/ (per-tick receipts) Activation API (Hono) —> Pre-Hype UI
[ lineagewar.xyz — Live Scoreboard ]
lineagewar.xyz
LINEAGE WAR #14
Last tick: 2m ago
TRIBE A
847
TRIBE B
692
CONTESTED SYSTEMS
Aridia-7
Syndicate-3
Cloud Ring
ON-CHAIN
Move Smart Contracts
WarRegistry, WarAdminCap, PhaseConfig, SystemConfigVersion, WarResolution — all shared objects on Sui. War creation, tribe registration, phase publishing, scheduling, and auto-resolution via wallet-signed transactions.
VERIFIER
Event-Driven Scoring
Single Node.js process. Auto-discovers wars from chain events. Per-tick: queries assembly locations via GraphQL, resolves ownership through 3-batch pipeline, evaluates rules, commits to PostgreSQL, writes atomic public artifacts.
ADMIN
Showrunner Control
React + Sui wallet admin panel. Create wars, register tribes, publish phases, set assembly filters, schedule end, manage editorial display. POST /notify triggers immediate verifier rediscovery. The Orchestrator's instrument panel.
SCOREBOARD
CRT War Terminal
React scoreboard consuming live verifier output. Tribe score cards with spring animations, area charts tracking territorial control over time, interactive systems grid. Full CRT aesthetic — the war rendered as a terminal readout.
COMPOSABILITY

WarResolution objects live on-chain as permanent shared objects. The prediction market reads them directly to settle bets. No oracle. No settlement delay. Result published = market settles. The Orchestrator does not judge who won. The chain does.

// 04 — PREDICTION MARKET
The Economic Instrument

Prediction markets are the Orchestrator's kiln made economic. Players stake capital on outcomes they believe will happen. The market forces convergence on truth. Wrong predictions are costly. Correct ones profit. This is pressure that separates signal from noise.

The Orchestrator does not need to judge who won. The market does it for him. Consensus under pressure. This is the experiment.

19
Move Modules
3
Resolution Tiers
Sponsored Gas
Relay
N-Outcome
CPMM

orchestrator.lineagewar.xyz/markets

Frontend (React + TypeScript + Tailwind v4) /markets, /markets/create, /markets/:id, /portfolio, /airdrop | Mysten DApp Kit + Sponsored Txs Off-Chain Services Gas Relay — validates tx shape, sponsors approved calls Phase Bot — detects expired COMMIT/REVEAL phases, triggers tally | Sui Testnet On-Chain: prediction_market Move package Market Core pm_market, pm_trading, pm_math, pm_position Resolution pm_resolution, pm_dispute, pm_source Voting pm_sdvm (commit-reveal), pm_staking Config pm_registry, pm_policy, pm_rules, pm_admin Token suffer.move (coin, treasury, minting) Support pm_faucet, swap_pool, pm_treasury, pm_events, pm_view
AMM
Constant-Product Market Maker
CPMM pricing in pure Move integer arithmetic. Ceiling division on buy, floor division on sell — pool always wins rounding, solvency guaranteed by invariant. Binary markets use closed-form math; N-outcome markets use binary search over the product invariant. Scales to 16 outcomes.
SDVM
Schelling DVM — Native in Move
Commit-reveal stake-weighted voting adapted from UMA, built natively on Sui. Owned commit records enable parallel submissions; shared vote rounds coordinate tally. Three escalation tiers: optimistic oracle, appointed resolvers, tokenholder vote. Wrong voters get slashed. Correct voters earn from the slash pool.
GAS RELAY
Sponsored Transactions
Part of the deployed trust boundary, not convenience infrastructure. Validates transaction shape against policy, rejects non-conforming calls, sponsors approved ones. Users need zero SUI to participate. The relay is why the airdrop works — new players claim $SUFFER without owning gas.
SAFETY LEVERS
Progressive Decentralization
Every admin capability is documented, instrumented, and paired with measurable removal criteria. Emergency cap separated from admin cap — different keys, different powers. Review windows on emergency actions prevent unilateral kills. Bootstrap levers, not evidence of finished decentralization.
ECONOMIC ALIGNMENT

Correct votes are profitable. Wrong votes are costly. Committing but not revealing incurs a 10x penalty. The Schelling Point ensures convergence: if the truth is obvious, you vote for it, because you expect everyone else to do the same. Prediction markets are not speculation. They are pressure chambers for reality.

// 05 — $SUFFER AIRDROP
Liquidity for Riders of the Frontier

A prediction market without liquidity is a theory. $SUFFER (SFR) exists to make participation immediate. The gas relay sponsors every transaction. The faucet distributes starter capital. The result: a Rider connects their EVE Vault wallet, claims 100,000 SFR, and begins trading outcome shares within minutes — zero SUI required, zero friction, zero excuses.

The Orchestrator does not give you money. He gives you stake. What you do with it is the experiment.

$SUFFER (SFR)

orchestrator.lineagewar.xyz/airdrop

CLAIM YOUR $SUFFERING
$SUFFER (SFR)

The collateral token for all market activity. Starter claim of 100,000 SFR via the on-chain faucet, with 10,000 SFR daily returns. Gas-sponsored claims through the relay — players need only their EVE Vault wallet tied to their Frontier Rider. The faucet ran as a live campaign, distributing from a pool of 4.9M SFR.

// 06 — OPEN SOURCE
The Composable Game Engine

Every component is open-source and reusable. Not just the code — the methodology. Character-driven game modes on composable blockchain infrastructure. MIT licensed. Fork and deploy.

WAR ENGINE
lineagewar-OSS
11 Move modules, event-driven verifier, admin panel, CRT scoreboard, activation API, pre-hype landing page. Complete territorial control game mode. Fork and deploy for any Frontier event.
PREDICTION MARKET
predictionmarket-OSS
19 Move modules, React frontend, gas relay, phase bot. CPMM AMM, Schelling DVM dispute resolution, $SUFFER token, sponsored transactions. Plug into any on-chain outcome.
EMERGED FROM THIS
lead-producer-OSS
Full-stack AI product team framework that emerged from building the Orchestrator. 57 specialist skills: 34 roles, 12 review teams, 10 workflows. One entry point, structured analysis, coordinated agentic development. Used to build, review, and harden every component in this stack.
DESIGN SYSTEM
CRT Terminal Kit
Shared design tokens across all frontends. IBM Plex Mono, dark phosphor background, mint text, scanlines, vignette, grain, grid background. Zero border-radius everywhere. The aesthetic is the character — fork the tokens and the fiction comes with them.
// 07 — ASPIRATIONS
The Autonomous Agent

The Orchestrator is currently operated by a human. The aspiration: when the system runs itself — when an AI generates Ukases, creates game modes, sets bounties in $SUFFER — that is when the experiment is complete. All grounded in the kernel: the Orchestrator wants death on the Frontier because death is the kiln. Without real loss, there is no pressure. Without pressure, nothing crystallizes. The autonomous agent is not a convenience — it is the only way the experiment scales past what one Rider can run.

NOW
Two-Tribe Lineage War + Binary Markets
On-chain territorial control with prediction market overlay. Human-operated showrunner. Live on Sui testnet.
NEXT
N-Tribe Wars + Production Readiness
Contracts and verifier already support N tribes and N-outcome CPMM (2–16 outcomes). Next: multi-tribe scoreboard UI, GraphQL batching stress tests under concurrent load, verifier reliability hardening, and mainnet deployment preparation. The architecture is built — the constraint is operational confidence.
HORIZON
Autonomous Content Generation
AI agent that generates Ukases, creates game modes, sets bounties in $SUFFER, produces commentary. The character bible becomes the system prompt. The editorial display API becomes the output channel.
VISION
Player-Generated Game Modes
Bounties in $SUFFER. Community-authored war rules — racing, 1v1 duels, territorial sieges, economic control challenges. Multiple concurrent wars with independent prediction markets. The kiln, open to all.
"The Keep archives. The Feral Net digests. I am trying to determine whether anything survives either process."
— The Orchestrator
// 08 — LEARNINGS
What the Kiln Taught Us
NARRATIVE-FIRST
Character as Architecture
The Orchestrator's ideology drove every product decision — from the war engine to the token design. Same monospace font, same phosphor palette, same scanline grain across every surface. When the character and the system design are the same thing, coherence comes free. The fiction was not wrapped around tools. The tools are the fiction.
CHAIN-NATIVE
Build for Verification
Every war result is a permanent on-chain object. The prediction market reads the resolution directly. No oracle. This isn't ideology — it's making outcomes composable so the system runs without referees.
PROBABILISTIC
Markets as Truth Discovery
Prediction markets turn conviction into price signals. Participants who are wrong lose capital; participants who are right profit. The Lineage War is the bootstrap mechanism — it creates real on-chain outcomes for the market to price, giving the system something to bet on from day one.
PROGRESSIVE
Honest Decentralization
Tracer bullet first. Ship with admin levers, document every one, instrument usage, publish the criteria for removing each. Be honest about what is centralized and why. Open-source the code so the community can verify and help close the gap.
AGENTIC
AI as Collaborator
This entire system was co-created by a human-AI team during a hackathon. Claude wrote contracts, reviewed architecture, red-teamed economics. The result: lead-producer — 57 specialist skills that emerged from the process of building this stack.
ɱѿʀ-VII
No human or artificial agents were damaged beyond recovery during this process. Discomfort was however sustained. On the Frontier, that is called progress.
CCP GAMES • EVE FRONTIER • SUI • MARCH 2026
